description: Routing eval — validates intent-based skill selection. Prompts use natural team language, not technical keywords. Each case reflects how a real team member would phrase the request.

cases.prompt: Create a favorites feature for the app
Build a new app that tracks workouts and calories
This file is getting too big, help me split it into proper layers
Add a settings screen where users can edit their profile
Agrega una pantalla de historial de pagos
How should I structure the analytics service that multiple features share?
Add a new column to store the user's avatar URL
The transaction list should refresh automatically when data changes
Set up the database for the project
How do I fetch all entries between two dates?
The data isn't persisting between app launches
Necesito guardar el progreso del usuario localmente
Write tests for the WorkoutViewModel
The app crashes randomly, I think it's a threading issue
I'm getting a Sendable warning and I don't know how to fix it
How do I create a fake version of the repository for tests?
Add test coverage for the payment flow
I have a data race warning on this actor, how do I fix it?
Does the home screen match the Figma design?
Something looks off after my last change, can you check?
Take a screenshot of all the main screens before I open the PR
The spacing on this card looks wrong
cases.notes: Ambiguous — could be design-system. But 'looks wrong on running app' = ios-visual. If they said 'the component has wrong spacing', ios-design-system would be closer.

cases.prompt: Run visual regression on all screens
Set up the API client to call our backend
Add deep link support so users can share a link to a specific item
The token expires and the app doesn't refresh it automatically
I need a privacy manifest for App Store submission
Create a reusable card component for the design system
Add a Liquid Glass effect to the navigation bar
Set up the color tokens and typography for the app
Add offline support to the Notes feature
cases.notes: Feature + persistence → ios-architect wins because the scope is the whole feature. ios-architect's scaffold includes persistence patterns.

cases.prompt: The button color is wrong
cases.notes: Running app issue → ios-visual. If they said 'the Button component uses the wrong color token', ios-design-system would be closer.

cases.prompt: Quiero que la app funcione sin internet
cases.notes: Spanish. Scope is a feature/app capability → ios-architect. If they already have the feature and just need the DB layer, ios-persistence.
